title: Quantification of the total and water-extractable pentosan content in wheat flour using a higher-throughput version of the phloroglucinol colorimetric assay
author: Hernández-Espinosa, Nayelli
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: In the present study, we report a modification of the Douglas phloroglucinol colorimetric assay for the analysis of water-extractable and total arabinoxylan (AX) content in wheat whole meal and refined four. This method greatly improves the efficiency, throughput, and repeatability of the original protocol, being ideal for screening a relatively large number of wheat lines within a breeding program. Arabinoxylans represent the most abundant pentosans in wheat grain constituting around 85% of the total non-starch polysaccharides. Apart from their structural function in the plant, AX strongly influences wheat processing and end-use quality and constitutes the major source of dietary fibers in wheat grains.

Arabinoxylans represent the most abundant pentosans in wheat grain constituting around 85% of the total non-starch polysaccharides. Apart from their structural function in the plant, AX strongly influences wheat processing and end-use quality and constitutes the major source of dietary fibers in wheat grains.
